meta.title: Restaurant energy monitoring case studies
meta.description: UK and European hospitality examples for the restaurant energy monitoring guide. Mix of Greenways sustainability map entries and published benchmarks.

cases.summary: Flagship kitchen ventilation control at a Wetherspoon pub — proof-of-concept that scaled to a 654-site UK rollout.
cases.detail:
**Key findings:** CHEETAH DCKV targeted cookline ventilation without disrupting service. Measured kWh reduction supported the business case for chain-wide deployment.

**Payback:** About **1.9 years** on measured savings.

**Source:** Greenways sustainability map · Quintex portfolio.

cases.summary: Chain-wide monitoring and ventilation optimisation across hundreds of UK pub kitchens — one of Europe's largest hospitality energy programmes.
cases.detail:
**Why scale matters:** Cross-site benchmarking showed which kitchens ran extraction and HVAC outside service hours.

**Technology:** IoT-linked ventilation control with measured M&V at pilot sites before rollout.

**Source:** Greenways sustainability map · Quintex.

cases.summary: Ventilation and kitchen energy controls across Premier Inn and Beefeater restaurant operations — portfolio-level visibility before capex.
cases.detail:
**Approach:** Sub-metering and schedule controls on HVAC and extraction in restaurant zones tied to occupancy patterns.

**Outcome:** Documented kWh reduction supports SECR reporting and upgrade prioritisation.

**Source:** Greenways sustainability map.

cases.summary: A 60-cover London pub monitored refrigeration, extraction and HVAC. Overnight extraction after close was the biggest discovery.
cases.detail:
**Key findings:** Walk-in cooler cycling every 19 minutes vs a normal 12 — a failing door seal flagged by monitoring. Overnight HVAC and extraction accounted for **23%** of electricity.

**Payback:** Subscription covered within **4 months** in this illustrative scenario.

**Source:** Composite · Envigilance / Best.Energy benchmarks.

cases.summary: EnergyPlus modelling found a 20% refrigeration and kitchen efficiency gain delivered 15.7% carbon reduction — the highest-impact single intervention modelled.
cases.detail:
**Monitoring role:** Sub-metering identifies which equipment is inefficient — monitoring targets the right 20% improvement.

**Source:** Peer reviewed · Energies (MDPI), March 2025.

cases.summary: A European Burger King franchise operator implemented monitoring across sites, recovering €5,500 per year with payback in under six months.
cases.detail:
**What monitoring revealed:** HVAC outside hours, overnight lighting in back-of-house, and refrigeration setpoints consuming excess energy.

**Approach:** IoT sensors with subscription-based monitoring and guaranteed minimum savings.

**Source:** Spacewell / Nemetschek case study.

cases.summary: Three Funchal kitchens had electricity, water and waste monitoring deployed — air conditioning was the largest previously hidden drain.
cases.detail:
**Key insight:** The kitchen with HVAC monitored had **38%** of aggregated energy explained vs ~25% in others.

**Data frequency:** 1–5 Hz logging revealed spikes invisible on standard metering.

**Source:** Published · PMC / FIK dataset (2023).

cases.summary: IKEA Netherlands store restaurants implemented smart energy systems to meet Dutch sustainability grant requirements and cut kitchen loads.
cases.detail:
**Approach:** Monitoring on kitchen ventilation and refrigeration before equipment upgrades — measured baseline for ISDE-style reporting.

**Payback:** About **2 years** on measured savings.

**Source:** Greenways sustainability map.

cases.summary: IoT monitoring across 100+ Scandinavian food retail sites — refrigeration, HVAC and lighting — with cross-site benchmarking.
cases.detail:
**Why scale matters:** Outlier sites using 40% more energy than peers were investigated systematically.

**Technology:** Wireless IoT per site with failure alerts — no disruption to trading hours.

**Source:** Best.Energy case portfolio.

cases.summary: A large restaurant group cut greenhouse gas emissions by 8% through monitoring and scheduling alone — before any equipment replacement.
cases.detail:
**The insight:** Behaviour and scheduling changes enabled by monitoring drove the reduction — the monitoring system was the intervention, not only diagnostics.

**Source:** Budderfly (2025).

cases.summary: Demo-style monitoring view for a compact city kitchen — cookline gas, refrigeration and overnight standby visible on one dashboard.
cases.detail:
**Illustrative only:** Figures mirror the guide's demo dashboard — pair with **Greenways buildings dashboard** or **sensor dashboard** for portfolio-style KPIs.

**Next step:** Edwardo can map sensor priorities for a real site once you have a bill baseline.
